metadata.changes:
Change 1: Replace:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of Triples about food into a single fluent sentence.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
with:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of Triples about food into a single fluent sentence.
      Accepts either Triple objects or 3‑element tuples.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Normalize input: allow plain tuples (subject, predicate, object)
      if isinstance(triples[0], tuple) and len(triples[0]) == 3:
          triples = [Triple(*t) for t in triples]

diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Food. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: country - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | country | Spain)
Predicate: ingredient - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | ingredient | Almond)
Predicate: region - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | region | Andalusia)
Predicate: division - Example triple: (Almond | division | Flowering plant)
Predicate: family - Example triple: (Almond | family | Rosaceae)

Predicate: course - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | course | Italian meal structure)
Predicate: dishVariation - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | dishVariation | "Onion, garlic, black pepper, chili")
Predicate: mainIngredient - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | mainIngredient | "Tomatoes, guanciale, cheese, olive oil")
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Andalusia | leader | Susana Díaz)
Predicate: alternativeName - Example triple: (Asam pedas | alternativeName | "Asam padeh")

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about food into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a single triple into a phrase
    def triple_to_phrase(t: Triple) -> str:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        # Expanded natural language mappings for all supported predicates
        if p == "country":
            return f"{s} is from {o}"
        if p == "ingredient":
            return f"{s} contains {o}"
        if p == "region":
            return f"{s} originates from {o}"
        if p == "division":
            return f"{s} belongs to the division {o}"
        if p == "family":
            return f"{s} is in the family {o}"
        if p == "order":
            return f"{s} is classified under the order {o}"
        if p == "class":
            return f"{s} belongs to the class {o}"
        if p == "genus":
            return f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}"
        if p == "course":
            return f"{s} is served as a {o}"
        if p == "capital":
            return f"The capital of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "leader":
            return f"The leader of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "leaderTitle":
            return f"The title of the leader of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "alternativeName":
            return f"{s} is also known as {o}"
        if p == "creator":
            return f"{s} was created by {o}"
        if p == "creatorOfDish":
            return f"{s} was created by {o}"
        if p == "mainIngredient":
            return f"The main ingredients of {s} are {o}"
        if p == "dishVariation":
            return f"{s} can vary with {o}"
        if p == "servingTemperature" or p == "served":
            return f"{s} is served {o}"
        if p == "year":
            return f"{s} was introduced in {o}"
        if p == "carbohydrate":
            return f"{s} contains {o} of carbohydrates"
        if p == "fat":
            return f"{s} contains {o} of fat"
        if p == "protein":
            return f"{s} contains {o} of protein"
        if p == "servingSize":
            return f"The serving size of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "related":
            return f"{s} is related to {o}"
        if p == "similarDish":
            return f"{s} is similar to {o}"
        if p == "isPartOf":
            return f"{s} is part of {o}"
        if p == "currency":
            return f"The currency of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "demonym":
            return f"The demonym for {s} is {o}"
        if p == "language":
            return f"The language spoken in {s} is {o}"
        if p == "officialLanguage":
            return f"The official language of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "ethnicGroup":
            return f"The ethnic group of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "foundationPlace":
            return f"{s} was founded in {o}"
        # Default generic phrasing for any unspecified predicate
        return f"{s} {p} {o}"

    # Build a fluent sentence by grouping predicates per subject
    from collections import defaultdict

    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    subject_phrases = []
    for subject, preds in subject_map.items():
        # Build phrase list; first keeps full subject, others become relative clauses
        full_phrases = []
        for idx, t in enumerate(preds):
            phrase = triple_to_phrase(t).rstrip(".")
            # If not the first predicate, strip leading subject if present to form a relative clause
            if idx > 0 and phrase.startswith(t.subject + " "):
                phrase = phrase[len(t.subject) + 1 :]
            # Ensure relative clauses start with a connector
            if idx > 0 and not phrase.lower().startswith(("which", "that", "who", "whose", "where", "when")):
                phrase = "which " + phrase
            full_phrases.append(phrase)

        # Combine predicates for the same subject into a fluent clause
        if len(full_phrases) == 1:
            combined = full_phrases[0]
        else:
            combined = ", ".join(full_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + full_phrases[-1]
        subject_phrases.append(combined)

    if not subject_phrases:
        return ""

    # Combine different subjects into one sentence
    if len(subject_phrases) == 1:
        sentence_body = subject_phrases[0]
    else:
        sentence_body = ", ".join(subject_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + subject_phrases[-1]

    # Capitalize first character and ensure final period
    sentence = sentence_body[0].upper() + sentence_body[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
